Event: Convinces Erin to drink despite her vulnerability
Explanation: He gets social/romantic leverage while increasing her impairment and risk.

Event: Offers to take drunk Erin home
Explanation: He gains proximity and apparent goodwill while putting her in a dependent situation that worries others.

Event: Drops Erin off safely instead of exploiting her
Explanation: He preserves his image and avoids trouble while ensuring her safety.

Event: Gives Erin advice before leaving
Explanation: He offers guidance with no apparent cost to himself.

Event: Andy follows them, suspecting Robert’s intentions
Explanation: He expends time/stress to protect her, gaining little direct benefit.

Event: Gets drunk out of depression about his divorce
Explanation: His heavy drinking worsens his own condition without helping anyone.

Event: Shuts down another branch while drunk
Explanation: He damages the company and livelihoods based on impaired judgment, with little to no gain.

Event: Tries to hit on Nellie while drunk
Explanation: He risks reputation and workplace fallout while making her uncomfortable.

Event: Hijacks Oscar’s “It gets better” video
Explanation: He redirects a serious message to serve his own persona, undercutting its intended impact.

Event: Drinks coconut-and-penis–flavored energy drink and laments coconut
Explanation: His trivial, self-imposed discomfort serves no one and slightly harms his own enjoyment.

Event: Kisses Andy on the lips as a goodbye
Explanation: He creates awkwardness and potential HR issues with no clear benefit to himself.

Event: Invites Jim and Pam to his home for a threesome
Explanation: He seeks sexual gratification leveraging his position, putting them in an uncomfortable power dynamic.

Event: Cancels the proposed threesome after re-evaluating power dynamics
Explanation: He avoids ethical/professional fallout and spares them coercive pressure.

Event: States he is “never uncomfortable” while watching Andy’s private talk
Explanation: He maintains dominance at the cost of Andy’s ease and privacy.

Event: Convinces Jo to give him her job in one conversation
Explanation: He gains a top position while outmaneuvering a capable boss to her and the firm’s detriment.

Event: Provokes rivalry between office members
Explanation: He keeps control and psychological leverage while harming cohesion and comfort.

Event: Gives employees tasks he then works to undermine
Explanation: He reinforces his mystique and control but wastes others’ work and morale.

Event: Introduces himself with different names to different people
Explanation: He gains opacity and power through confusion, mildly disorienting others.

Event: Walks around the office starting intense, creepy conversations
Explanation: He entertains/informs himself while unsettling staff.

Event: Obsessively spends an entire Halloween party learning everyone’s deepest fears
Explanation: He gains psychological leverage while initially making people vulnerable.

Event: Improvises a ghost story that uses those fears to unify and release tensions
Explanation: He applies his knowledge to create catharsis and group cohesion, enhancing his status and helping them.

Event: Makes bizarre post-divorce decisions that drive Sabre into the ground
Explanation: His erratic leadership harms the company and endangers his own role and reputation.

Event: Convinces David Wallace to give him a new job with Eastern European gymnasts
Explanation: He secures a comfortable next role while placing vulnerable youths under a dubious “helper.”

Event: Tries to get Ryan and Gabe into a three-way at his house party
Explanation: He pursues sexual gratification that makes subordinates uncomfortable.

Event: Attempts to start a naked pool orgy with employee guests
Explanation: He risks severe reputational and legal fallout while distressing his staff.

Event: Cites a Sesame Street film about paper production as a qualification
Explanation: He uses an odd example to present relevant knowledge, mildly benefiting both his candidacy and their evaluation.

Event: Goes along with Jo-endorsed retail store idea despite thinking it will fail
Explanation: He protects Jo’s legacy and his image while allowing a plan he expects to fail.

Event: Blames failure of retail idea on an employee “botching the execution”
Explanation: He protects his own standing by shifting responsibility onto a subordinate.
